The recommendations of the Ohio legislative commits
sion appointed to investigate the high cost of living,
have passed practically unnotced yet its treatment of
economic and domestic subjects is Of the very highest
Importance to the whole nation.


What relief is there finally in bowling about high
costs, cold storage and combinations controlling prices
of foodstuffs, when conditions such set forth
the two paragraphs following:


l. The bureau OF and labor reports that the

ucts $l400O000O WORTH against $21 100000 in 1906
In short, rapidly approaching time when
WILL NOT PRODUCE ENOUGH TO EAT, and that
old theory about Uncle Sam feeding the world will be but
haZy dream.


2. The Washington authorities put the immigration
for the fiscal year 1910 at 100000 The first nine
months show 667,949. Taking last month a sample,
there came to us $500 Germans 3oS6 Italians, 22oO3
Polish and 1906 Greeks, mostly agricultural folk.


1t safe to say that 80 per cent of these people
will be nonproducers-that is they will locate in the
cities OF mines, virtually BECOMING SLAVES IN ALL
SAVE THE NAME


Here we have threatened shortage in production and
big increases in nonproducng consumers. 1t plainly
the burning of the economic candle at both ends to
nothing of the great national moral issues involved.


Tie Ohio statesmen referred to suggest remedies They
wou! have the state maintain agricultural schools and
experimental farms in each county.


They would have labor bureau to furnish laborers to
farmers.


They would have the moral physical and domestic adj
vanrages of country life taught in all the public schools


They would push to the front everything having ten
COUNTRY TO THE FARMS instead of to the city.


We venture to say that very few thinking patriots
be found who do not feel that the future of our nation
and its highest ideals depend upon serious powerful
along the lines indicated by these Ohio invest
gators.


We have got to have teaching and practicng of
